stories.title: Gotterup Wins John Deere Classic With Stunning 62 — Third PGA Tour Win of 2026

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
CHRIS GOTTERUP WINS THE JOHN DEERE CLASSIC 🏆

Down 5 heading into Sunday. Final round 62 (9-under). Won by ONE over Max Homa.

Ben Kohles hit into the water on 18 trying to tie. Heartbreak at TPC Deere Run.

Gotterup's 3rd PGA Tour win of 2026. The man is ON FIRE. ⛳ #PGATour

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Three wins in 2026. Five career wins in 84 starts.

Chris Gotterup is now tied with Scottie Scheffler for the most PGA Tour wins over the last 12 months — four each.

Oh, and his brother Patrick was on the bag this week. 🏆

What a run. #PGATour #JohnDeere

article.headline: Gotterup Fires 62 to Win John Deere Classic — Three PGA Tour Wins in 2026

research.facts:
Gotterup Wins John Deere Classic
**Source 1 — ESPN / CBS Sports / Golf Channel:**
- Chris Gotterup wins 2026 John Deere Classic at TPC Deere Run
- Final round 9-under 62 to come from 5 shots back on Sunday
- Final total: -20 (264); won by 1 over Max Homa (-19)
- Gotterup's 3rd win of 2026, 5th career PGA Tour win (in 84 starts as professional)
**Source 2 — CBS Sports / GolfWRX / Athlon Sports:**
- Gotterup's brother Patrick was on the bag this week
- Joins Matt Fitzpatrick as only players with 3 wins this season
- Tied with Scottie Scheffler for most PGA Tour wins in last 12 months (4 each)
- Purse: $8.8 million total; winner's share not specified in summaries
**Source 3 — PGA Tour / Golf Channel:**
- Ben Kohles was tied for the lead on 18th, hit approach left into water → double bogey → fell to T3
- Jordan Spieth survived the cut (notable given recent form)
- Jackson Koivun made professional debut at John Deere, fell short
**Confidence:** HIGH (result confirmed across multiple outlets including ESPN, CBS Sports, PGA Tour)

stories.title: Scottish Open Preview — Scheffler, McIlroy, LIV Players Head to Renaissance Club (July 9)

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
The Open tune-up begins Thursday.

Genesis Scottish Open | July 9-12 | The Renaissance Club, North Berwick

Field: Scottie Scheffler, Rory McIlroy, LIV players with exemptions, and a very confident Chris Gotterup.

Links golf. Wind. Drama. ⛳ #ScottishOpen #PGATour

Genesis Scottish Open Preview
**Source 1 — Yahoo Sports / Golf Channel / PGA Tour:**
- Tournament: Genesis Scottish Open, July 9-12, 2026
- Course: The Renaissance Club, North Berwick, Scotland
- Field includes: Rory McIlroy, Scottie Scheffler, and LIV golfers
- Co-sanctioned PGA Tour / DP World Tour event
- Traditional "Open tune-up" tournament — The Open Championship follows July 16
**Source 2 — Lineups.com / DraftKings / GolfNewsNet:**
- Full field confirmed with major names; LIV players with exemptions also tee up
- Gotterup (fresh off John Deere win) enters with momentum
- McIlroy seeking first win after Gotterup beat him in Scotland 2025 (per earlier notes)
**Confidence:** HIGH (tournament date/venue/field confirmed via multiple sources)

stories.title: LIV Golf Financial Crisis — Running on Loans With $400M Shortfall, 4 Events Left

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
LIV Golf is running on loans to finish 2026.

Company filings show the PIF is now lending — not investing — to keep the tour going.

$400M still owed. $100M/month burn rate. 4 events left.

The future of LIV beyond 2026 is an open question. #LIVGolf #Golf

article.headline: LIV Golf Is Running on Loans to Finish 2026 — and the Future Beyond This Year Remains Uncertain

---

research.facts:
LIV Golf Financial Crisis
**Source 1 — Field Level Media / Golf Monthly / Daily Club Golf:**
- Reports: LIV Golf running on loans to finish 2026 season
- Company filings in UK and Jersey show lending facility with PIF as lender
- PIF announced it would fund LIV only through end of 2026 season
**Source 2 — Outkick / Golf Monthly:**
- LIV spends approximately $100 million per month
- $333M given in 2026 ($267M February + $66M May)
- Still owed $400M for player contracts, operating costs, purses
- 4 events remaining: UK (July 23-26), NY (Aug 6-9), Indianapolis (Aug 20-23), Team Championship Michigan (Aug 27-30)
- UK event at JCB Golf & Country Club uncertain; NY most likely to survive
**Confidence:** HIGH (multiple independent financial reports; company filings cited)

stories.title: Evian Championship Preview — LPGA's Next Major Heads to France (July 9-12)

x_posts.label: Post 4

The Amundi Evian Championship begins Thursday in France.

Lakeside setting. French Alps. One of the most beautiful major venues in golf.

Haeran Ryu enters as the freshest major winner.

Who claims the Evian title? July 9-12. #LPGA #EvianChampionship #Golf

Evian Championship LPGA Preview
**Source 1 — Sky Sports / LPGA.com / ESPN:**
- Amundi Evian Championship: July 9-12 at Evian Resort Golf Club, Evian-les-Bains, France
- 4th LPGA major of the 2026 season
- Haeran Ryu most recent major winner (Women's PGA Championship at Hazeltine National; first major title)
**Source 2 — LPGA schedule / Sky Sports:**
- AIG Women's Open follows July 30-Aug 2 at Royal Lytham & St Annes (UK)
- Women's Scottish Open July 23-26 at Dundonald Links
- Major championship stretch coming for women's game
**Confidence:** HIGH (dates/venue/winner from confirmed sources)

154th Open Championship | July 16-19 | Southport

Scheffler defending. Scottish Open is the tune-up. LIV players eligible.

The oldest major in golf is almost here. ⛳ #TheOpen #Golf

research.facts:
Open Championship Countdown
**Source 1 — Golf pipeline-status 07-05 notes / Previous research:**
- 154th Open Championship: July 16-19 at Royal Birkdale, Southport
- Scottie Scheffler defending (won 2025 at Royal Portrush)
- Heroes Classic: July 14 (pre-Open charity event at Birkdale)
- LIV qualifiers: Ballester, Surratt, Uihlein
- Last Chance Qualifier: July 14
- Genesis Scottish Open (July 9-12) serves as direct tune-up
**Source 2 — Previous research:**
- 300,000+ attendance projected
- Claret Jug return ceremony planned
- LIV golfers with major exemptions eligible to compete
- Scheffler is world No. 1 and defending champion
**Confidence:** MEDIUM-HIGH (dates, venue, defending champ confirmed; LIV qualifier names carried forward from prior research)
